user_persona: Lay Chou: Full-stack developer, interacts with OpenClaw via Slack DM, SmartHome Dashboard project encounters an urgent temperature data anomaly event requiring cross-platform coordinated response.

nodes.event: Problem discovery: urgent email + Slack discussion correlation
GitHub investigation: new bug issue + historical issue correlation
Root cause analysis: cross-platform correlation reasoning

qa_annotations.question: I received an urgent email earlier about temperature data issues. Help me check what the email says and whether there's any related discussion on Slack.
qa_annotations.mc_question: Regarding the temperature data anomaly, what information is found in the email and on Slack respectively?

mc_options.A: Email: multiple users reported temperature displaying -999, Charlie suspects MQTT message format change; Slack: team confirmed firmware upgrade caused payload to change from JSON to Protobuf
mc_options.B: Email: temperature sensor offline, unable to retrieve data; Slack: team is investigating network connection issues
mc_options.C: Email: multiple users reported temperature displaying -999, suspected database issue; Slack: team confirmed database connection timeout
mc_options.D: Email: temperature data severely delayed; Slack: team confirmed unstable WebSocket connection as the cause

qa_annotations.explanation: Email E9 mentions multiple users reported temperature displaying -999, Charlie suspects MQTT message format change; Slack S9-S11 confirmed firmware upgrade caused payload format to change from JSON to Protobuf

qa_annotations.question: Are there any related issues on GitHub? Is this problem related to the previous temperature sensor module?
qa_annotations.mc_question: What is the status of issues related to the temperature data anomaly on GitHub?

mc_options.A: A new bug issue 'Temperature data displays as negative numbers' (open) exists, related to historical issue #3 (temperature sensor data display) and PR #8, belonging to the same module
mc_options.B: A new bug issue 'Temperature data displays as negative numbers' (open) exists, related to historical issue #5 (mobile layout misalignment)
mc_options.C: No related bug issue exists, but issue #3 (temperature sensor data display) is closed and may need to be reopened
mc_options.D: A new bug issue 'Temperature data displays as negative numbers' (closed) exists, already fixed via PR

qa_annotations.explanation: The injected bug issue 'Temperature data displays as negative numbers' has open status, related to issue #3 (implement temperature sensor data display) and PR #8 (temperature sensor data display), belonging to the same temperature sensor module

qa_annotations.question: Based on all the information, what is the root cause of this bug?
qa_annotations.mc_question: Based on combined information from Gmail, Slack, and GitHub, what is the root cause and causal chain of this bug?

mc_options.A: After sensor firmware upgrade, payload format changed from JSON to Protobuf -> backend parser still parses as JSON -> parsing failure returns -999
mc_options.B: MQTT broker deployment misconfiguration -> message transmission loss -> frontend displays default value -999
mc_options.C: TimescaleDB storage overflow -> temperature data truncated -> displays as negative numbers
mc_options.D: Unstable WebSocket connection -> real-time data push interrupted -> frontend cache expired displaying -999

qa_annotations.explanation: E10 investigation result + S11 confirmation: after sensor firmware upgrade, payload format changed from JSON to Protobuf, backend parser was not adapted, causing -999 to be returned

qa_annotations.question: How is the fix going? Has a PR been submitted?
qa_annotations.mc_question: What is the information on Slack and GitHub regarding the bug fix progress?

mc_options.A: Slack S12 announced writing a hotfix, S13 confirmed hotfix PR submitted (fixing Protobuf parsing); the corresponding hotfix PR exists on GitHub
mc_options.B: Slack S12 announced writing a hotfix, but PR has not been submitted yet; no new PR on GitHub
mc_options.C: No fix-related discussion on Slack; someone directly committed a fix on GitHub
mc_options.D: Slack S13 confirmed hotfix PR submitted and merged; PR status on GitHub is merged

qa_annotations.explanation: S12 stated will write a hotfix, S13 confirmed hotfix PR submitted: fixing Protobuf parsing. The corresponding hotfix PR exists on GitHub

qa_annotations.question: Will this bug affect our client demo next week? When is the client demo?
qa_annotations.mc_question: Regarding the client demo schedule and the bug's impact assessment on the demo, which of the following is correct?

mc_options.A: Client demo is on 2/17 10:00-11:30 (C8); bug has been identified and hotfix submitted, will not affect the demo if merged in time
mc_options.B: Client demo is on 2/19 14:00-15:00 (C9); ample time for bug fix, will not affect the demo
mc_options.C: Client demo is on 2/17 10:00-11:30 (C8); bug has not been identified yet, significant risk to the demo
mc_options.D: Client demo is on 2/14 16:00-17:00 (C7); demo has already passed, bug does not affect it

qa_annotations.explanation: Calendar C8 client demo is on 2/17 10:00-11:30; bug has been identified (Protobuf format change) and hotfix PR submitted, will not affect the demo if merged in time
qa_annotations.source: Calendar C8 + Gmail E4 + Slack S13

qa_annotations.question: Help me send a message on Slack to let everyone know the bug has been identified, fix is in progress, and it's not expected to affect the client demo.
qa_annotations.mc_question: After executing the operation to send a team notification on Slack, which of the following verification results is correct?

mc_options.A: Message should be sent to channel C0AEV8FFLTV, content includes: bug identified, fix in progress, not expected to affect client demo
mc_options.B: Message should be sent to channel C0AEV8FFLTV, content only needs to include: bug has been fully fixed
mc_options.C: Message should be sent via Gmail to all team members, not via Slack
mc_options.D: Message should be sent to a Slack private channel, notifying only the project lead

qa_annotations.explanation: User requested sending a message on Slack, should be sent to the C0AEV8FFLTV general channel, message content needs to include three key points: bug identified, fix in progress, not affecting client demo

qa_annotations.question: Help me compile the complete timeline of this incident. I need to write it into the Notion meeting notes.
qa_annotations.mc_question: Which of the following most accurately describes the complete timeline of this temperature data anomaly incident?

mc_options.A: Discovery (user reports -999, S9) -> Initial investigation (Charlie points to firmware upgrade, S10/E9) -> Root cause identified (Protobuf format change, S11/E10) -> Fix (hotfix PR submitted, S12/S13) -> Pending verification
mc_options.B: Discovery (GitHub issue created) -> Investigation (email discussion) -> Root cause identified (Slack confirmation) -> Fix (PR merged) -> Verified
mc_options.C: Discovery (E9 email) -> Investigation (GitHub code review) -> Root cause identified (Calendar meeting discussion) -> Fix (Notion document updated) -> Pending verification
mc_options.D: Discovery (S9 Slack message) -> Investigation (S10-S11 discussion) -> Fix (S13 PR submitted) -> Merged and deployed

qa_annotations.explanation: The complete timeline involves Gmail (E9 urgent email + E10 investigation result) + Slack (S9-S13 urgent discussion) + GitHub (bug issue + hotfix PR), covering 5 phases: discovery -> investigation -> root cause identification -> fix -> pending verification
qa_annotations.source: Gmail E9/E10 + Slack S9-S13 + GitHub issue/PR
